summary: Descriptive overview of liquidateddamages in substantive law: enforceability standard, penalty distinction.
key_points: enforceability standard is a fundamental concept in contract law.
Legal standards for liquidateddamages are established through case law and statutory interpretation.
Courts apply specific tests to determine liquidateddamages in litigation.
The doctrine of liquidateddamages has evolved through Supreme Court precedents.
Understanding liquidateddamages requires analysis of both historical context and modern applications.
philosophy_guard_statement: This fragment describes existing legal doctrines and their applications without advocating for normative changes.
